title: Code of Hammurabi
summary: One of the earliest recorded law codes, setting precedents for written governance.

title: Athenian democratic reforms
summary: Cleisthenes expands citizen assemblies and lays foundations for participatory governance.

title: Qin unification of China
summary: Qin Shi Huang centralizes power, standardizes script and measures, and builds early Great Wall sections.

title: Edict of Milan
summary: Imperial decree legalizes Christianity and sets precedent for religious toleration policies.

title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
summary: Deposition of Romulus Augustulus marks transition toward medieval polities in Western Europe.

title: Magna Carta
summary: Charter compels the English crown to acknowledge limits on royal authority and uphold certain rights.

title: Black Death pandemic
summary: Bubonic plague kills tens of millions, reshaping economies, labor relations, and social orders.

title: Gutenberg printing press
summary: Movable type accelerates book production, literacy, and the spread of ideas across Europe.

title: Columbian Exchange
summary: Sustained contact between the Americas and Afro-Eurasia transfers crops, diseases, people, and ideas.

title: Protestant Reformation
summary: Reform movements challenge Catholic authority, triggering religious wars and new confessional states.

title: Scientific Revolution
summary: Empirical methods and publication norms emerge, anchored by works like Newton's Principia.

title: American Revolution
summary: Thirteen colonies declare independence, testing Enlightenment ideals in governance and rights.

title: French Revolution
summary: Monarchy falls, declarations of rights spread, and Europe enters decades of ideological conflict.

title: Industrial Revolution
summary: Mechanization, coal power, and factory labor transform productivity and urban life.

title: World War I
summary: Industrialized warfare redraws borders and strains empires, prompting new international bodies.

title: World War II
summary: Global conflict ends with Axis defeat, genocide trials, nuclear weapons, and superpower rivalry.

title: United Nations and Universal Declaration of Human Rights
summary: Postwar institutions form to reduce conflict and codify shared human rights standards.

title: Global decolonization
summary: Dozens of colonies gain independence, reshaping global governance and economic alignments.

title: Space Race and Moon landing
summary: Cold War competition drives satellite technology and culminates in the Apollo 11 landing.

title: U.S. Civil Rights Movement
summary: Grassroots organizing and landmark laws dismantle formal segregation and expand voting rights.

title: Publication of the Pentagon Papers
summary: Leaked study reveals U.S. decision-making in Vietnam War, fueling transparency debates.

title: Watergate investigations
summary: Reporting and congressional inquiries expose executive misconduct, leading to presidential resignation.

title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
summary: Peaceful protests and policy shifts open the border, accelerating the end of the Cold War division.

title: World Wide Web becomes public
summary: Tim Berners-Lee opens web standards, enabling rapid global information sharing.

title: Truth and Reconciliation Commission
summary: Public hearings document apartheid-era abuses and create a model for transitional justice.

title: WikiLeaks diplomatic cables
summary: Release of U.S. diplomatic cables exposes candid assessments and fuels debates on transparency.

title: Arab Spring uprisings
summary: Protests and political upheavals challenge entrenched governments with varied outcomes.

title: Snowden surveillance disclosures
summary: Leaks about mass surveillance spark legal reforms, corporate encryption shifts, and privacy debates.

title: COVID-19 pandemic
summary: Global outbreak triggers public health mobilization, economic disruption, and rapid vaccine development.
